ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Schmidt, via Zoom, for and with Robert T. Roddick, Personal Representative.

At the court's query, Messrs. Schmidt and Roddick offer advocacy as to why they do not believe that the Estate of Donna Roberts is entitled to statutory compensation for her years of service as Administrator. Mr. Roddick reports on Ms. Robert's tenure as Administrator and of his belief that she intended to waive compensation.

After additional discussion, the court advises that it is satisfied with the testimony offered and the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-24-2021 Continued To Dec-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Robert Roddick, Esq. for Michael Kirwan;
Randy Schmidt, Esq. for Robert Roddick, Administrator.

The Court states that the matter is on for close of the estate, and that it doesn't have either petition on file. Mr. Schmidt requests additonal time noting that they are addressing years of extraordinary fees, and that Mr. Schmidt was out for two weeks. The Court notes that was the almost the same information that it heard at the last hearing. Mr. Schmidt requests fifteen additional days stateing that he had more difficulty putting his extraordinary fees together, that he had been away, and that while in Mexico he was hospitalized for two weeks.

At the conclusion of counsels' comments, the Court continued the matter to December 22, 2021, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court states that either a petition on report of status of administration or a petition for final distribution should be on file by December 15, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-13-2021 Continued To Nov-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randy Schmidt appeared by zoom for Robert Roddick (Administrator) by zoom.

Mr. Schmidt stated that the property sold, that one provision in the sale was that the sole heir could remain in the property until November or forfeit $100,000.00 and that the heir got out. Mr. Roddick stated his client is very happy, that his client has moved into a clean surrounding and that his client is looking forward to getting his inheritance. Mr. Schmidt requested a two to three-week continuance. Status on close of estate is continued to November 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Schmidt is to file his petition prior to the hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S. Park. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3965-3967 24th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randal Schmidt, via ZOOM, for Robert Roddick, Administrator; Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, for William Casey, Trustee.

The court confirms with Mr. Bernhard of his filing in support of Trustee's request for reimbursement which is pending in the court's e-filing queue. The court then confirms with Mr. Schmidt that Trustee's request will not impede the petition to confirm sale of real property.

The sale was confirmed at $1,585,750 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required. The court has contacted Mr. Schmidt to advise that an updated order confirming the sale of real property is required.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Schmidt appeared by zoom for and with Robert Roddick by zoom.
Michael Kerwin also present by zoom.

The Court noted that this case has a lengthy history and that it is now being requested that Mr. Roddick become the Administrator. Mr. Schmidt stated that the three of them (Mr. Schmidt, Mr. Roddick and Mr. Kerwin) had a meeting earlier this week, that they all agreed that it would more efficient for Mr. Roddick to become the Administrator, that Mr. Roddick is familiar with the estate and that once Mr. Roddick is appointed, they will sell the property. Mr. Schmidt also stated that Mr. Roddick has already contacted Tim Hawko (Realtor), that all of the other owners have agreed to use Mr. Hawko and that Mr. Kerwin who is the tenant is prepared to move out once the property is sold. In addition, Mr. Schmidt stated that the trust proceeding settled, that nothing is going on other than to sell the property and that there is no need to have the Court enforce the settlement agreement. The Court noted that Mr. Roddick is the nominee of the Heir and stated that if Mr. Roddick is appointed, things need to move along as quickly as possible. Mr. Roddick stated that he understands. Mr. Schmidt stated that he believes Mr. Kerwin has waived bond and that Mr. Kerwin is the only Heir. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition and for the issue of bond to be addressed. Status on close of estate is set for September 15, 2021 at 9:00, a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of bond is also set for September 15, 2021 at 9:00, a.m., in Dept. 204. The Report on Status of Administration on calendar for March 23, 2021 is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall B. Schmidt, Esq., counsel for and with Donna Roberts, Administrator (Mr. Schmidt appearing by video conference, Ms. Roberts appearing by telephone).

The Court notes want of receipt of a verified Petition or Status Report for the estate. Mr. Schmidt offers calendaring error for the missed appearance last time and reports that the Petition is in a posture to be filed by the end of the week.

After further discussion, the Court continues the matter to December 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for filing of the petition. The Court reminds counsel that a proposed form of Order must also be submitted. If the Petition is filed and proposed Order lodged with the Court prior to hearing, no appearance need be made on December 7th.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randy Schmidt for Donna Roberts (Administrator).

Ms. Schmidt reported that they have reached a tentative settlement in the litigated 850 petition in San Mateo involving an accounting of the trust. Mr. Schmidt also stated that the settlement agreement was approved on January 10th, that part of the settlement is that cash in the amount of $325,000.00 will go into this estate, that a one quarter interest in the 24th Street property is going to be sold and that he is happy to do a status report. Petition To Determine Ownership To Property; Double Damages; Conversion; Constructive Trust; Accounting; Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bernhard for (Respondent) William Casey.

The Court acknowledged that Mr. Schmidt has not come in, and asked Mr. Bernhardt about the case in San Mateo. Mr. Bernhard reported that they are set for trial in January, that they will do mediation in November, that they hope to resolve the issues, that the petition in San Mateo is identical to this one, that he is not sure why both have been filed and that Mr. Schmidt has indicated it is due to the statute of limitations for one of the parties. The matter was recessed in hopes that Mr. Schmidt would appear.
9:35 a.m. the matter is recalled, Mr. Schmidt is not present. The Court states that Mr. Bernhard is present and that it is prepared to take the petition off calendar. Mr. Bernhard confirmed again that there is a trial date in January, that Mr. Schmidt made no mention of continuing this hearing and that he himself had suggested taking this petition off calendar but Mr. Schmidt had reservations. The Court took the Petition to Determine Ownership to Property; Double Damages; Conversion, etc. Off Calendar.

Judge:Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
